subject: Mathematics
title: Achilles and the Tortoise
description: A quick, practical guide to Achilles and the Tortoise.

hook.text: The fastest runner in Greece can never catch a slow tortoise—at least according to Zeno's logic.

buildup.text: Achilles gives the tortoise a head start. By the time he reaches its spot, it has moved ahead a bit.

discovery.text: He reaches that new spot, but the tortoise has moved again. The gap shrinks but never vanishes.

twist.text: In reality, Achilles passes the tortoise easily. The infinite steps add up to a finite time.

climax.text: The paradox was resolved 2,000 years later by the mathematics of convergent infinite series.

punchline.text: Infinite steps can take finite time. That's the magic of convergence.

quiz.question: How is Zeno's Achilles paradox resolved mathematically?
options: The infinite series of distances converges to a finite sum
